data.content_ref: inline:The volcanic cooling mechanism (639a93) and the prolonged drought mechanism (58e798) are mutually exclusive as primary triggers for the same observed harvest failures because the climatic signatures they produce are contradictory: a volcanic winter yields a short-term (1-3 year) cooling pulse with reduced evaporation, whereas a multi-decadal drought requires a sustained shift in precipitation patterns driven by long-term atmospheric circulation changes. REFUTED IF paleoclimatic proxies (e.g., tree-ring width, speleothem δ18O) show the cooling event and the drought are temporally coincident and causally linked rather than separate, incompatible climatic episodes.
